2008: Olympic alernate

- Posted a 0.00 ERA with a 1-0 record with six strikeouts in 5.0 innings pitched
2005: Silver medalist at World Cup
- Allowed no earned runs in 3.2 IP, striking out six
2004: Gold medalist at 1st World University Softball Championship and member of Schutt Elite Team at Canada Cup and Champions Cup
- Posted a 0.00 ERA with a 4-0 record striking out 49 batters and giving up only seven hits in 28.2 IP (World University)
- Struck out a team-high 62 batters in 51.0 innings for a 0.96 ERA (Canada Cup/Champions Cup)

AT ARIZONA
Led Arizona to the National title in 2006, pitching every single inning for the Wildcats
- Most Outstanding Player of NCAA WCWS
- Broke strikeout record in a WCWS with 64
Two-time Top three finalist for the USA Softball Collegiate Player of the Year (2004,2006)
Two-time First-team All-American
Four-time All Pac-10 Team member

GETTING TO KNOW HOLLOWELL

I am a leap year baby. I am taller than my older sister. I have two brothers and two sisters. I love to cook especially for other people. My favorite food is Mexican food. I love being able to relax and hang out with my close friends. I love singing even though I am tone deaf. My birthday is February 29, 1984. I enjoy live music, especially live guitar music. I like random excursions where I just jump in my car and drive somewhere fun. I like camping and being surrounded by nature. I love pictures even though I am really bad at pulling my camera out and taking them for myself. I love to laugh and people that make me laugh. I like trying out new restaurants when I can pull myself away from my favorites. I love spoiling my little brother and sister. My parents are Doug and Suzanne Hollowell. I prefer drinking out of a straw. I love to dance even though I don’t have any rhythm. I enjoy working out. I love being at the softball field. I love the smell of rain and watching lightning storms. I am scared to ski or snowboard. I am overly careful at times. When I’m bored I play on the Internet and look up random things. I don’t like insects, especially spiders. Target and Buckle are my two favorite stores. I love to wander the aisles at grocery stores or Target. Even if I am not going to buy much, I go down every aisle. I am laid back about almost everything in life but can be very picky and anal about a few certain things. I am sarcastic. I like MAC makeup. I love getting pedicures. I love playing Guitar Hero with my number one.

bio.education: Camden High School (Camden, N.J.) ‘91 Norfolk State University, ’96, Communication and Media Studies; University of Phoenix, MBA
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • A former track & field athlete at Norfolk State University, Holmes knew she was destined for a different career path after taking a job at a telecommunications company after college. After a tragic train accident in 2001 that resulted in the amputation of her left leg below the knee, she discovered the Paralympic Movement after her surgeon gave her a pamphlet with hopes of sport being an outlet to be active again and achieve the career she always wanted. Three months later, Holmes received her first walking prosthetic and one year after her accident, she participated in her first race at Disney's Wide World of Sports in Orlando, Florida, where she currently trains.
  • Runs the April Holmes Foundation, Inc. a non-profit organization assisting people with physical and learning disabilities with scholarships and medical equipment
  • Co-authored her first book with Brian Tracy entitled, “The Winning Way” and is set to release her second book “Stop Limping Thru Life, Start Running”
  • Assisted First Lady Michelle Obama on the “Let’s Move” Campaign
  • Serves as an athlete ambassador on the United States Anti-Doping Agency and as a mentor for Classroom Champions
  • First female on the athlete roster representing The Jordan Brand and was the inspiration behind Air Jordan’s 2009 signature shoe with APT technology
  • Enjoys listening to musical artists ranging from Usher, Jay Z and Mary J. Blige to Phil Collins
  • Three weeks prior to her accident, she started on her master’s degree at Drexel University
  • Won numerous accolades, including the NCAA’s Inspiration Award, U.S. Olympic Committee’s Spirit Award, U.S. Paralympics Mentor of the Year and National Association of Black Journalist Pioneer Award.
Paralympic Experience
  • 4-time Paralympian; 3-time Paralympic medalist (1 gold, 2 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, (200m T44 - Women), 10th (100m T44 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, bronze (100m T44 - Women), 8th (200m T44 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2008, (200m T44 - Women, Long Jump T44 - Women), gold (100m T44 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Athens 2004, bronze (Long Jump T44/46 - Women), 6th (100m T46 - Women), 7th (200m T46 - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2015 – 6th (200m T44 - Women), 7th (100m T44 - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m T44 - Women 2006, 2011, 2013, 2015; 200m T44 - Women 2006, 2011, 2013, 2015; 400m T46 - Women 2006; Long Jump T44 - Women 2006
  • Medals: 4 (2 gold, 2 bronze)
    • Gold – 2006 (100m T44 - Women, 200m T44 - Women)
    • Bronze – 2013 (100m T44 - Women); 2011 (100m T44 - Women)

Most Notable
-> 2012 Olympic Games Silver Medalist and Best Spiker (.379 hitting efficiency)
-> 2012 USA Volleyball Female Indoor Athlete of the Year
-> MVP at 2011 FIVB World Grand Prix where USA won the gold medal
-> Best Spiker at 2011 FIVB World Cup where USA won the silver medal

Major International Competitions
2012 - FIVB World Grand Prix Preliminary Round...Olympic Games (Silver).
2011 - FIVB World Grand Prix (Gold)...NORCECA Championship (Gold)...FIVB World Cup (Silver).
2010 – FIVB World Grand Prix (Gold)...Tour of Brazil...FIVB World Championship (Fourth).

Did You Know
-> Was cut from her volleyball team as a 13-year-old
-> Gave birth to first child, daughter Keitany, on Dec. 12, 2013
-> Participated in 2008 U.S. Olympic Trials in high jump; personal best was 1.98m
-> Won the 2009 NCAA Indoor High Jump championship and the 2009 NCAA Outdoor High Jump championship for the third time at University of Texas, becoming just the second female in NCAA history to win three NCAA Outdoor High Jump titles and the first female high jumper to sweep the indoor and outdoor crowns since 2004
-> Sister Marshevet placed fifth in the 200 meters at the 2008 Olympics
-> Father Ricky was a sixth-round draft pick of San Antonio Spurs
-> Logan Tom was her volleyball role model as a young player (Los Angeles Times, Aug. 9, 2012)
-> Trained with the U.S. Women's National Team in Spring 2008 in hopes of making the 2008 Olympic Games roster
-> Scored international personal-high 39 points vs. China on Nov. 16 in FIVB World Cup
-> Transitioned from a college outside hitter to an elite international opposite hitter in three weeks time, earning a starting role a week into her first international tournament (FIVB World Grand Prix)
-> Played collegiate volleyball at University of Texas, earning American Volleyball Coaches Association First-Team in 2008 and 2009

The Big Blurb
Can Destinee come back from a season off in 2013 after giving birth to her first child and be the same dominant player that was respected as one top volleyball players in the world? “My daughter has brought so much joy to my life,” Hooker-Coulter said after the birth of her daughter Keitany. “It only motivates me to get back on the court and play not only for me, but more for her. I love her so much. She's my heartbeat. I'm motivated more than ever and nothing is going to stand in my way.”

The Quote
"I'm more of an energizer," Hooker said in relation to her past sport, high jumping. "In high jump you're by yourself and I hated that. Having to walk back and forth like that waiting for my coach. I like receiving energy and giving out energy. I think volleyball was a great choice."

Olympic Experience:

  • 2012: 15th

How he qualified for London 2012: Hornsby, 26, took the long way to Olympic qualification. He won the Men’s K1 200m event at the 2011 Sprint National Championships in Gainesville, Ga. on Aug. 6. But it was teammate Ryan Dolan who qualified the Olympic boat quota slot for the U.S. at the 2011 Pan American Games in Guadalajara, Mexico on October 28. Hornsby pulled even with Dolan in the Olympic selection process by winning the 2012 U.S. Olympic Team Trials in Oklahoma City on April 20, forcing a race-off between the two at the first two 2012 World Cups. Hornsby clinched the spot for himself with a 14th place finish at the 2012 World Cup No. 2 in Duisburg, Germany on May 27.

Four Years Wiser: Hornsby, attended the Beijing 2008 Olympic Games as a training partner for U.S. Olympian Rami Zur. Hornsby returns to the Olympic stage - this time as an Olympian - after outlasted teammates Zur and Ryan Dolan in the long 2012 selection process. Joining Hornsby in London will be his girlfriend Becky Holliday, who qualified for her first Olympics in pole vaulting. Hornsby is the first Olympian produced by the Lanier Canoe and Kayak Club, which has won 10 National Championships. Lake Lanier hosted the canoe/kayak and rowing events at the Atlanta 1996 Olympic Games.

Girlfriend: 2012 U.S. Olympic pole vaulter Becky Holliday
Nickname: "Timmay"
Job: tutor
Hobbies: Kitesurf, surf, biking, and travel

In the News: TeamUSA.org

Son of Roger and Patti Hornsby, Tim was born on July 20, 1986 in Atlanta, Georgia. He Attended Lakeside High School in Atlanta and graduated in 2004. Tim has been on the water since age 11 when he learned to paddle at spring break camp. After high school, Tim was accepted to the University of California San Diego, Jacob's School of Engineering.

He was first coached by Tony Hall in 1997 at the Lanier Canoe and Kayak Club. Tim’s Breakout year was in 2003, when he went to the World Junior Championships and placed 10th in the K2 1000-meter race at the age of 17. While still competing with the Junior National Team, Tim was in the K4 boat that placed 4th at Olympic Trials in 2004.

In 2006 at the Pan American Championships he placed 3rd in the K1 200-meter race. Then in 2007, Tim placed 2nd in the 4x100 meter relay at the World Cup in Szeged, Hungary. Tim has really begun to grow into a world class competitor. He started out 2008 by placing 1st at Olympic Trials in K2 500-meter race, as well as the K4 1000-meter race. He went on to compete in the World Cup circuit and placed 8th in the K2 500-meter race, and 8th in the K2 1000-meter race in Szeged, Hungary.

Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games London 2012, 15th (K1 200m - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2014 – 11th (K1 200m - Men)
  • Years of participation: K1 200m - Men 2009, 2010, 2014; K2 200m - Men 2011; K1 4 x 200m Relay - Men 2009, 2010; K4 1000m - Men 2005, 2006, 2007, 2009; K4 200m - Men 2005, 2006, 2007; K4 500m - Men 2006
  • Top finish: 9th – 2007 (K4 200m - Men)

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Olympic Games London 2012, 5th (Team - Men), 6th (Horizontal Bar - Men), 10th (Rings - Men), 56th (Pommel Horse - Men), 68th (Parallel Bars - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2008, silver (Horizontal Bar - Men), bronze (Team - Men), 9th (Individual All-Around - Men), 17th (Floor Exercise - Men, Rings - Men), 22th (Parallel Bars - Men), 48th (Pommel Horse - Men)
Career Highlights
  • Won three NCAA titles with Oklahoma
  • 2006 NCAA all-around champion
  • 2008 USA Gymnastics Men's Athlete of the Year
  • Three-time American Cup all-around champion
  • 2010 World all-around bronze medalist

Fun Facts: Horton's favorite meal is BBQ chicken, refried beans, potato salad, and a Coke ... Horton began gymnastics in 1990 ... In 2009, Horton married Haley DeProspero, a former gymnast for the University of Oklahoma
